days.title: Arrival in Madrid
days.subtitle: Exploring the heart of Spain

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Catalonia Atocha
timeline.detail: Arrive at your hotel, a short walk from Atocha train station. Freshen up and get ready to explore the city.

timeline.title: Visit the Prado Museum
timeline.detail: Spend your afternoon admiring masterpieces from European artists like Velázquez and Goya in one of the world's most prestigious museums. Depending on the day, consider booking a guided tour for deeper insights.

timeline.title: Dinner at Sobrino de Botín
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Spanish meal at the oldest restaurant in the world, famous for its roast suckling pig. Prepare for a lively atmosphere filled with history.

timeline.title: Evening Stroll in Plaza Mayor
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk around Plaza Mayor, enjoying the vibrant ambiance and perhaps grab a dessert from a nearby café. A great way to wrap up the day.

timeline.title: Relax at the hotel
timeline.detail: Return to your hotel to unwind after a busy day of exploring. Get a good night's sleep to prepare for tomorrow’s adventures.

localTips: Consider booking Prado tickets in advance at www.museoprado.es to skip the line.
Pick up a Madrid Tourist Ticket for unlimited transport on the metro and buses for 1, 2, or 3 days.
Explore local tapas bars near your hotel for late-night dining options.

days.title: Art and Culture Day
days.subtitle: Discover the modern and classical scenes

timeline.title: Breakfast at Café de Oriente
timeline.detail: Start your day with a delicious breakfast overlooking the Royal Palace. Enjoy traditional Spanish breakfast options while sipping freshly made coffee.

timeline.title: Royal Palace of Madrid
timeline.detail: Explore the stunning Royal Palace, the official residence of the Spanish royal family. Don't miss the lavish rooms and the impressive gardens.

timeline.title: Lunch at Mercado San Miguel
timeline.detail: Indulge in a variety of gourmet tapas and local delicacies at this bustling market filled with vibrant food stalls. A perfect spot to taste many Spanish dishes.

timeline.title: Visit Reina Sofia Museum
timeline.detail: View modern art masterpieces, particularly Picasso's Guernica, at this contemporary art museum. The architecture itself is a blend of old and new.

timeline.title: Dinner at La Ramesa
timeline.detail: Experience innovative Spanish cuisine at this stylish restaurant. The tasting menu highlights local ingredients and creative dishes.

timeline.title: Evening Flamenco Show
timeline.detail: Attend a captivating flamenco show at a traditional tablao. Enjoy the passion of the dancers and the authenticity of the experience.

localTips: Get free entry to the Royal Palace on Mondays (check hours)!
Try to arrive early for the Flamenco show to get the best seats.
Ask locals for their secret tapas spots near Mercado San Miguel.

days.title: Culinary Exploration
days.subtitle: Indulging in flavors of Madrid

timeline.title: Breakfast at La Pantoja
timeline.detail: Enjoy a light breakfast with local pastries and coffee at this beloved neighborhood café before your final day of exploring.

timeline.title: Cooking Class Experience
timeline.detail: Join a Spanish cooking class where you'll learn to make traditional dishes like paella. Enjoy a hands-on experience preparing the meal yourself.

timeline.title: Lunch of Your Creations
timeline.detail: Sit down to enjoy lunch made with the dishes you created during your cooking class. Pair it with local wine for an authentic experience.

timeline.title: Explore Chueca Neighborhood
timeline.detail: Visit the vibrant Chueca neighborhood known for its trendy shops and lively atmosphere. Don't forget to check out local artisan boutiques.

timeline.title: Dinner at El Club Allard
timeline.detail: Enjoy a fine dining experience at this Michelin-starred restaurant, where each dish is an artistic creation showcasing contemporary gastronomy.

timeline.title: Nightcap at a Rooftop Bar
timeline.detail: End your trip with a relaxing drink at a rooftop bar with spectacular views of the Madrid skyline. Reflect on your culinary journey.

localTips: Book your cooking class in advance at www.cookingclassmadrid.com.
Chueca is known for its nightlife; consider returning after dinner!
Make dinner reservations at El Club Allard as it can get full.

hotels.name: Catalonia Atocha
hotels.note: Conveniently located 5min walk from Atocha station

budget.note: Estimated total for meals over 3 days

urgent.label: Book Cooking Class
urgent.note: Spots fill up quickly; book at www.cookingclassmadrid.com

urgent.label: Reserve Flamenco Show
urgent.note: Highly recommended to pre-book for good seats.
